item.title: Cloud state writers missing try-catch on atomic file writes
item.intent: Trust mode and command queue writes must never fail silently. A trust mode switch to paranoid that fails silently leaves the agent in a less-restrictive security posture with no indication — this is a security failure, not a reliability failure.
item.description: Four cloud module state writers perform atomic writes (writeFileSync + renameSync) without try-catch: src/cloud/heartbeat/reporter.ts:56-61 (heartbeat state), src/cloud/trust-modes/switch.ts:70-75 (trust mode state), src/cloud/commands/queue.ts:63-68 (command queue), src/cloud/fleet/discovery.ts:60-65 (fleet registry). BUG-027 fixed the read side (JSON.parse) but missed the write side. A disk write failure silently discards the update — a trust mode switch to 'paranoid' could fail with no warning, leaving the agent in an unexpected state. Steps: (1) src/cloud/trust-modes/switch.ts:70-75 — wrap in try-catch and THROW on error (critical path — caller must know if trust mode change failed); (2) src/cloud/commands/queue.ts:63-68 — wrap in try-catch and throw (enqueue failure must propagate); (3) src/cloud/heartbeat/reporter.ts:56-61 — wrap in try-catch and console.warn (best-effort, non-critical); (4) src/cloud/fleet/discovery.ts:60-65 — wrap in try-catch and console.warn (best-effort); (5) run cloud/ tests after each change. Scope: 4 files, ~8 additions. Note: trust-modes/switch.ts and commands/queue.ts must throw; reporter.ts and discovery.ts log-only.
